label: High sugar pattern
reason: Sugary drinks or sweet packaged foods appear repeatedly.
possibleConcern: May increase risk of energy crashes, cravings, and long-term lifestyle concerns.

label: Added sugar watch
reason: Added sugar is present in packaged snacks or breakfast foods.
possibleConcern: Could make daily sugar load higher than expected.

label: High sodium pattern
reason: Instant noodles, chips, or salty packaged foods were found.
possibleConcern: May increase risk of excess sodium intake if frequent.

label: Frequent fried food
reason: Fried snacks appeared in the scan or diary.
possibleConcern: Could increase calorie and oil load when eaten often.

label: Processed food load
reason: Packaged or instant foods are taking space from whole foods.
possibleConcern: May reduce fiber, micronutrient, and protein quality.

label: Low protein signal
reason: The pattern has quick carbs or snacks without enough protein.
possibleConcern: May affect fullness and balanced daily energy.

label: Refined flour signal
reason: Maida/refined flour appears in the item pattern.
possibleConcern: May crowd out whole grains if used frequently.

label: Palm/refined oil signal
reason: Packaged food may include palm oil or refined oils.
possibleConcern: Could add hidden fat load when eaten often.

label: Trans fat warning
reason: The label mentions trans fat or hydrogenated fat.
possibleConcern: Avoid frequent use; consider safer alternatives.

label: Artificial color watch
reason: The label mentions artificial colors or synthetic additives.
possibleConcern: May be better kept occasional, especially for children.

label: High spice watch
reason: The meal was marked high in spice.
possibleConcern: May cause discomfort for some people if frequent, especially with fried or outside food.

label: Ultra-processed food pattern
reason: Packaged snacks, sugary drinks, instant foods, or ready-to-eat items appear in the log.
possibleConcern: May increase hidden sugar, sodium, additives, and low-fiber intake when frequent.

label: Refined flour signal
reason: Maida/refined flour foods like naan, pizza, burger, momos, rolls, or bhature appear.
possibleConcern: May reduce whole-grain intake and affect fullness if frequent.

label: High fat pattern
reason: Creamy, buttery, fried, or oil-heavy foods appear in the day.
possibleConcern: May raise calorie load and heaviness when repeated often.

label: High calorie density
reason: The food item is calorie dense for a regular snack or meal.
possibleConcern: May make total daily energy intake higher than expected.

label: High carbohydrate load
reason: The meal is dominated by rice, bread, potato, noodles, or refined grains.
possibleConcern: May cause energy dips if not balanced with protein, fiber, and vegetables.

label: Low fiber pattern
reason: The log has processed/refined foods but few vegetables, fruits, dal, sprouts, or whole grains.
possibleConcern: May affect digestion, fullness, and gut-health patterns over time.

label: Low vegetable signal
reason: Few vegetable-rich foods were found in the day’s entries.
possibleConcern: May reduce micronutrient and fiber quality in the daily diet.

label: Sugary drink habit
reason: Cola, packaged juice, energy drink, milkshake, or sweet beverages appear.
possibleConcern: Liquid sugar may increase cravings and calorie load without fullness.

label: Late-night heavy meal
reason: A heavy, fried, or high-sodium item was logged late in the day.
possibleConcern: May affect sleep comfort or digestion for some people when frequent.

label: Outside food heavy day
reason: Restaurant, delivery, or street-food entries appear multiple times.
possibleConcern: May increase hidden oil, sodium, refined flour, and portion-size load.

label: Packaged snack load
reason: Chips, biscuits, namkeen, bars, or other packaged snacks are present.
possibleConcern: May add hidden salt, sugar, refined flour, and oils when eaten often.

label: Sweet/dessert watch
reason: Desserts, chocolates, or sweets appear in the diary.
possibleConcern: May increase daily sugar load if not portion-controlled.

label: Dessert frequency signal
reason: Dessert items were found in the daily/weekly food pattern.
possibleConcern: May be better kept occasional or paired with balanced meals.

label: Light option detected
reason: A low-calorie or lighter food/drink option appears in the log.
possibleConcern: Good sign when it supports balance, but avoid under-eating.

label: Fiber-positive food
reason: Fiber-rich foods like fruits, sprouts, dal, millets, oats, or vegetables appear.
possibleConcern: This supports fullness and digestion-friendly patterns.

label: Protein-positive food
reason: Protein-rich foods such as dal, paneer, eggs, chicken, fish, tofu, sprouts, or curd appear.
possibleConcern: This supports fullness, recovery, and balanced meals.

label: Whole-food positive signal
reason: Less processed foods such as home meals, fruits, dal, vegetables, or whole grains appear.
possibleConcern: This usually improves overall food quality when repeated.

label: Vegetable-positive signal
reason: Vegetable-rich meals or sides appear in the food log.
possibleConcern: This supports fiber, micronutrients, and better plate balance.

label: Fermented food positive signal
reason: Curd, idli, dosa, dhokla, chaas, or similar fermented foods appear.
possibleConcern: Can support meal variety and digestion-friendly patterns for many people.

label: Balanced meal signal
reason: The meal appears to include a better mix of carbs, protein, and/or vegetables.
possibleConcern: A positive pattern; keep portions and frequency balanced.

label: Sauce/condiment watch
reason: Sauces like ketchup, mayonnaise, or packaged dips appear.
possibleConcern: Can add hidden sugar, salt, and fat when used often.

label: Side-item watch
reason: Pickle, papad, chutney, or condiments appear as sides.
possibleConcern: Some sides can add sodium/oil; use them in controlled portions.

label: Street-food pattern
reason: Street-food items such as pani puri, sev puri, vada pav, or chaat appear.
possibleConcern: May increase sodium, refined flour, fried load, or hygiene uncertainty when frequent.

label: Restaurant meal pattern
reason: Restaurant meals or thalis appear in the food log.
possibleConcern: May include larger portions and hidden oil/salt; balance with lighter meals.

label: Drink choice watch
reason: A drink entry was detected.
possibleConcern: Choose water, chaas, unsweetened drinks, or coconut water more often than sugary beverages.

label: Breakfast quality signal
reason: A breakfast item was detected in the diary.
possibleConcern: A balanced breakfast with protein and fiber can improve daily food pattern.

label: Home-meal positive signal
reason: Home-style meals like dal, roti sabzi, khichdi, rajma, or curd rice appear.
possibleConcern: Usually easier to control oil, salt, and portion quality than outside food.

label: Healthy snack positive signal
reason: Snacks like makhana, roasted chana, sprouts, peanuts, or fruit appear.
possibleConcern: Can improve satiety and reduce packaged snack dependence.

label: Fruit-positive signal
reason: Whole fruit or fruit bowl appears in the food log.
possibleConcern: Whole fruit adds fiber and is usually better than packaged juice.

label: Packaged sweet watch
reason: Packaged sweet foods such as cream biscuits, chocolate, or sweet cereals appear.
possibleConcern: May add sugar, refined flour, and oils without much fullness.

label: Portion-heavy pattern
reason: Large meal items or multiple calorie-dense entries were logged together.
possibleConcern: May make daily calorie load higher than expected.

label: Repeated same-food pattern
reason: The same item appears repeatedly in recent logs.
possibleConcern: May reduce variety; rotate protein, vegetables, and whole grains.

label: Low-protein breakfast pattern
reason: Breakfast appears mostly carb/sugar based without enough protein.
possibleConcern: May reduce fullness and cause mid-day cravings for some people.

label: Hidden sugar signal
reason: Packaged drinks, flavored milk, cereals, sauces, or sweet snacks may contain hidden sugar.
possibleConcern: Could increase sugar load even when the food does not look like a dessert.

label: Hidden sodium signal
reason: Packaged, instant, restaurant, or sauce-based items may contain hidden sodium.
possibleConcern: May increase sodium intake if these foods repeat often.

label: Oil-load signal
reason: Fried, buttery, creamy, or packaged foods appear in the log.
possibleConcern: Could increase heaviness and calorie density when frequent.

label: Children snack watch
reason: Cream biscuits, sweet cereal, chips, chocolate, or sugary drinks appear.
possibleConcern: May be better kept occasional, especially for children.

label: Energy crash pattern
reason: Sugar/refined-carb heavy entries appear without enough protein or fiber.
possibleConcern: May lead to hunger or energy dips later in the day for some users.

label: Digestion discomfort pattern
reason: Fried, spicy, late-night, or heavy outside foods appear together.
possibleConcern: May cause bloating, acidity, or discomfort in sensitive users.

label: Frequent outside-food week
reason: Outside food appears repeatedly across recent diary days.
possibleConcern: May raise hidden oil, salt, and refined-flour exposure over time.
